name: Healthcare
description: Models for healthcare delivery including hospitals, clinics, specialty care, emergency services, pharmacy, and related medical operations.

name: Manufacturing
description: Models for discrete and process manufacturing, assembly lines, job shops, paint shops, and related production operations.

name: Logistics & Supply Chain
description: Models for warehousing, order fulfillment, cross-docking, freight, distribution networks, and supply chain operations.

name: Retail
description: Models for retail and consumer-facing operations including storefront queuing, checkout, fulfillment, and point-of-sale operations.

name: Food & Beverage
description: Models for quick-service and sit-down restaurants, commercial kitchens, drive-thru operations, and food-retail activities.

name: Transportation
description: Models for transportation systems including aviation, rail, maritime, urban transit, and last-mile delivery.

name: Telecommunications
description: Models for call centers, network traffic, service desks, customer support, and telecommunications infrastructure.

name: Financial Services
description: Models for banking operations, insurance claims, trading floors, branch operations, and financial infrastructure.

name: Government & Public Sector
description: Models for government and public services including DMV operations, courts, social services, and public-facing administration.

name: Energy
description: Models for utilities, power plants, grid operations, and energy distribution including field service dispatch for utility workers.

name: Foundational Patterns
description: Domain-agnostic classical DES models that serve as educational building blocks and validation benchmarks — M/M/1, job shop, inventory policies, batch-and-queue, and similar canonical patterns that appear across industries.
